heading: Five-year limited eligibility of qualified aliens for Federal means-tested public benefit

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law and except as provided in subsections (b), (c), and (d), an alien who is a qualified alien (as defined in ) and who enters the United States on or after , is not eligible for any Federal means-tested public benefit for a period of 5 years beginning on the date of the alien’s entry into the United States with a status within the meaning of the term “qualified alien”.section 1641 of this titleAugust 22, 1996

children.label: A
children.text: An alien who is admitted to the United States as a refugee under section 207 of the Immigration and Nationality Act [].8 U.S.C. 1157

children.label: B
children.text: An alien who is granted asylum under section 208 of such Act [].8 U.S.C. 1158

children.label: C
children.text: An alien whose deportation is being withheld under section 243(h) of such Act [] (as in effect immediately before the effective date of section 307 of division C of ) or section 241(b)(3) of such Act [] (as amended by section 305(a) of division C of ).8 U.S.C. 1253Public Law 104–2088 U.S.C. 1231(b)(3)Public Law 104–208

children.label: D
children.text: An alien who is a Cuban and Haitian entrant as defined in section 501(e) of the Refugee Education Assistance Act of 1980.

children.label: E
children.text: An alien admitted to the United States as an Amerasian immigrant as described in section 1612(a)(2)(A)(i)(V) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: a veteran (as defined in section 101, 1101, or 1301, or as described in ) with a discharge characterized as an honorable discharge and not on account of alienage and who fulfills the minimum active-duty service requirements of ,section 107 of title 38section 5303A(d) of title 38

children.label: B
children.text: on active duty (other than active duty for training) in the Armed Forces of the United States, or

children.label: C
children.text: the spouse or unmarried dependent child of an individual described in subparagraph (A) or (B) or the unremarried surviving spouse of an individual described in clause (i) or (ii) who is deceased if the marriage fulfills the requirements of .2section 1304 of title 38So in original. Probably should be “subparagraph (A) or (B)”.2

children.heading: Exception for citizens of freely associated states
children.label: 3 Exception for citizens of freely associated states
children.text: An individual described in .section 1612(b)(2)(G) of this title

children.heading: Application of term Federal means-tested public benefit
children.label: c Application of term Federal means-tested public benefit

children.text: The limitation under subsection (a) shall not apply to assistance or benefits under paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: Medical assistance described in .section 1611(b)(1)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: Short-term, non-cash, in-kind emergency disaster relief.

children.label: C
children.text: Assistance or benefits under the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1751

children.label: D
children.text: Assistance or benefits under the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1771

children.label: E
children.text: Public health assistance (not including any assistance under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.]) for immunizations with respect to immunizable diseases and for testing and treatment of symptoms of communicable diseases whether or not such symptoms are caused by a communicable disease.42 U.S.C. 1396

children.label: F
children.text: Payments for foster care and adoption assistance under parts B and E of title IV of the Social Security Act [ et seq., 670 et seq.] for a parent or a child who would, in the absence of subsection (a), be eligible to have such payments made on the child’s behalf under such part, but only if the foster or adoptive parent (or parents) of such child is a qualified alien (as defined in ).42 U.S.C. 620section 1641 of this title

children.label: G
children.text: Programs, services, or assistance (such as soup kitchens, crisis counseling and intervention, and short-term shelter) specified by the Attorney General, in the Attorney General’s sole and unreviewable discretion after consultation with appropriate Federal agencies and departments, which (i) deliver in-kind services at the community level, including through public or private nonprofit agencies; (ii) do not condition the provision of assistance, the amount of assistance provided, or the cost of assistance provided on the individual recipient’s income or resources; and (iii) are necessary for the protection of life or safety.

children.label: H
children.text: Programs of student assistance under titles IV, V, IX, and X of the Higher Education Act of 1965 [ et seq., 1101 et seq., 1134 et seq., 1135 et seq.], and titles III, VII, and VIII of the Public Health Service Act [ et seq., 292 et seq., 296 et seq.].20 U.S.C. 107042 U.S.C. 241

children.label: I
children.text: Means-tested programs under the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 [ et seq.].20 U.S.C. 6301

children.label: J
children.text: Benefits under the Head Start Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 9831

children.label: K
children.text: Benefits under title I of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act [ et seq.].29 U.S.C. 3111

children.label: L
children.text: Assistance or benefits provided to individuals under the age of 18 under the Food Stamp Act of 1977 ( et seq.).17 U.S.C. 2011
an individual described in , but only with respect to the programs specified in subsections (a)(3) and (b)(3)(C) of ; orsection 1612(a)(2)(G) of this titlesection 1612 of this title
an individual, spouse, or dependent described in , but only with respect to the specified Federal program described in .section 1612(a)(2)(K) of this titlesection 1612(a)(3)(B) of this title
